data.title: STOCKS | Texas Stock Exchange Attracts Executives from Major Bourses
data.description: The Texas Stock Exchange, a new Dallas-based bourse supported by prominent figures such as Michael Dell and BlackRock, has successfully recruited executives from Nasdaq and the NYSE. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the exchange's strategic move to strengthen its leadership team by bringing in experienced professionals from established stock exchanges. This development marks a significant step for the Texas Stock Exchange as it seeks to establish itself in the competitive financial markets. The involvement of major asset managers underscores the potential impact of this new player in the industry.

data.title: U.S. Money Market Funds See Significant Inflow, ICI Reports
data.description: The Investment Company Institute (ICI) has reported a notable increase in money market fund assets in the United States. According to Jin10, for the week ending March 18, these assets grew by $38.68 billion, reaching a total of $7.86 trillion. This surge reflects a growing investor interest in money market funds, which are often considered a safer investment option during times of economic uncertainty. The increase in assets suggests a shift in investor sentiment towards more conservative financial instruments.

data.title: Evernorth Plans SPAC Listing with Significant XRP Holdings
data.description: Evernorth has announced its intention to launch with a substantial holding of at least 473 million XRP, valued at approximately $685 million, as part of its planned SPAC listing. According to NS3.AI, the company has secured over $1 billion to establish its XRP treasury. Evernorth aims to go public through a business combination with Armada Acquisition Corp. II, which is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker XRPN. The proposed transaction is subject to review by the SEC and requires shareholder approval. Evernorth plans to actively manage its treasury, seek DeFi yield opportunities, and initially expand its operations into Japan and South Korea.

data.title: Quantum Battery Prototype Charges in Femtoseconds
data.description: Australian researchers have developed a prototype of a quantum battery capable of charging in femtoseconds and storing energy for nanoseconds. According to NS3.AI, the device demonstrates increased charging speed with the addition of more molecules. However, the current prototype remains too small for practical consumer applications.

data.title: Israel to Halt Attacks on Iran's Major Gas Field, Netanyahu Says
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has announced that Israel will pause its attacks on Iran's major gas field. According to Jin10, this decision comes amid heightened tensions in the region. The move is seen as a strategic shift in Israel's approach to its ongoing conflict with Iran. Netanyahu's statement highlights the complex geopolitical dynamics at play, as both nations navigate their interests in the Middle East. The halt in attacks may provide a temporary reprieve in the escalating situation, though the long-term implications remain uncertain.

data.title: UK Aid Budget Cuts Impact Poorest Nations
data.description: The United Kingdom's significant reduction in its aid budget is set to affect some of the world's most impoverished and unstable countries. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns from campaigners and Members of Parliament about the potential decline in Britain's soft power. The cuts are expected to have far-reaching consequences, impacting the UK's influence and its ability to support global development initiatives. Critics argue that the reduction undermines the country's commitment to international aid and could exacerbate challenges faced by vulnerable nations. The decision has sparked a debate on the balance between domestic priorities and global responsibilities.

data.title: Bitcoin Holders Show Resilience Amid Market Decline
data.description: A recent survey of 1,006 American Bitcoin holders reveals a strong sense of resilience among investors despite a significant market downturn. According to NS3.AI, 69% of respondents neither sold nor planned to sell their Bitcoin holdings after the cryptocurrency experienced a 46% drop from its all-time high of $126,000 in early 2026. The survey also highlighted that 25% of participants took advantage of the lower prices by purchasing more Bitcoin, while only 8% were identified as panic sellers. This data suggests a prevailing confidence among the majority of Bitcoin investors, even in the face of substantial market volatility.

data.title: Jeff Bezos Seeks $100 Billion for AI-Driven Manufacturing Fund
data.description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is reportedly in discussions to secure $100 billion for a new investment fund aimed at acquiring manufacturing companies and integrating artificial intelligence into their operations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this initiative on the manufacturing sector. The Wall Street Journal reported that Bezos's plan involves leveraging AI to enhance efficiency and innovation within acquired companies. This move aligns with a growing trend of incorporating advanced technologies into traditional industries to boost productivity and competitiveness. Bezos's initiative could significantly influence the future landscape of manufacturing by fostering technological advancements and operational improvements.

data.title: FalconX Considers IPO Amid $8 Billion Valuation
data.description: FalconX, a prominent player in the cryptocurrency sector, is exploring the possibility of an initial public offering (IPO). According to NS3.AI, the company was last valued at $8 billion during its Series D funding round in June 2022. While FalconX has engaged in preliminary discussions with potential advisers, it has yet to officially appoint any banks to manage the offering. Cantor is among the financial institutions pitching for a role in the IPO process.

data.title: France's Foreign Minister to Visit Israel on Friday
data.description: France's Foreign Minister, Catherine Barro, is scheduled to visit Israel on Friday, according to Jin10. This visit comes amid ongoing diplomatic efforts in the region. The French Foreign Ministry announced the trip, highlighting the importance of strengthening bilateral relations and discussing regional security issues. Barro's visit is expected to include meetings with Israeli officials to address mutual concerns and explore avenues for cooperation. The visit underscores France's commitment to engaging with key partners in the Middle East to promote stability and peace.

data.title: Former Barclays CEO Jes Staley Faces Divorce Amid Epstein Controversy
data.description: Jes Staley, the former CEO of Barclays, is facing a divorce filed by his wife. Bloomberg posted on X that Staley stepped down from his position and was subsequently banned from the UK financial industry due to his association with Jeffrey Epstein. The developments mark a significant personal and professional setback for Staley.

data.title: Energy Infrastructure Destruction: Quick Damage, Lengthy Recovery
data.description: The destruction of energy infrastructure can occur within minutes, but the process of rebuilding it can take several months. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced in restoring such critical systems after damage. The rapidity of destruction contrasts sharply with the lengthy and complex efforts required for reconstruction, underscoring the vulnerability of energy networks to disruptions.

data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Discusses Hormuz Strait Aid Legal Constraints with U.S. President Trump
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister Satuki Katayama has engaged in discussions with U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the legal limitations surrounding Japan's aid in the Hormuz Strait. According to Jin10, the conversation focused on the challenges Japan faces in providing assistance in this strategically significant region. The Hormuz Strait is a critical maritime passage, and the legal constraints discussed could impact Japan's ability to contribute to international efforts in the area. The dialogue between the two leaders highlights the ongoing geopolitical considerations and the importance of international cooperation in maintaining security and stability in key global regions.

data.title: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu: Iranian Regime May Survive
data.description: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has expressed his belief that the Iranian regime might endure despite ongoing challenges. According to Jin10, Netanyahu's remarks come amid heightened tensions in the region, with Iran facing both internal and external pressures. The Israeli leader's comments highlight the complex geopolitical dynamics at play, as various nations continue to monitor Iran's political landscape closely. Netanyahu's statement reflects a cautious outlook on the future stability of Iran's government, suggesting that despite adversities, the regime could persist.

data.title: HYPE's Symmetrical Triangle Breakout Targets 68% Increase
data.description: HYPE is trading at approximately $39.71 following a symmetrical triangle breakout on the 2-day chart. According to NS3.AI, the measured move target is set at $52.27, indicating a potential increase of about 68%. Despite this breakout, the Chaikin Money Flow indicator is at -0.08, remaining below zero, which suggests a bearish divergence.

data.title: Senators Urge Release of Classified Stablecoin Study to Advance Legislation
data.description: Republican senators have reportedly urged Patrick Witt, Executive Director of the White House Crypto Council, to disclose a classified study by the Council of Economic Advisers on stablecoin yield. According to NS3.AI, the Senate may have approximately six weeks to advance the bill. Sources familiar with the report indicate that the findings are generally favorable for the cryptocurrency sector, suggesting that stablecoin rewards present limited risk to bank deposits. The ongoing dispute has stalled the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act since January, and making the study public could potentially resolve the current impasse.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Indices Rebound
data.description: The major U.S. stock indices have experienced a rebound, turning positive after initial declines. According to Jin10, this upward movement reflects a shift in market sentiment as investors reassess economic conditions and potential impacts on future monetary policy. The recovery in stock prices comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation and interest rates, which continue to influence market dynamics. Analysts suggest that this trend may persist as investors seek clarity on the economic outlook and central bank strategies.

data.title: Treasury Employees Resist Citizenship Verification Plan for Banks
data.description: Longtime employees at the U.S. Treasury Department are expressing opposition to the Trump administration's proposal requiring banks to verify citizenship information. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the resistance from within the department, which comes amid strong objections from lenders. The plan has sparked debate over its implications for privacy and the banking sector, with critics arguing it could lead to increased costs and operational challenges for financial institutions. The proposal is part of broader efforts by U.S. President Donald Trump's administration to tighten regulations and enhance security measures. However, the pushback from Treasury staff underscores the complexities and potential hurdles in implementing such policies.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Issues New General License Related to Russia
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department has announced the issuance of a new general license related to Russia, according to Jin10. This development comes as part of ongoing measures and regulations concerning economic activities involving Russia. The specifics of the license and its implications for businesses and individuals engaged in transactions with Russian entities have not been detailed in the announcement. The move reflects the U.S. government's continued focus on managing its economic and diplomatic relations with Russia amid broader geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Citigroup Adjusts 12-Month Targets for Bitcoin and Ether
data.description: Citigroup has revised its 12-month price targets for Bitcoin and Ether, setting Bitcoin at $112,000 and Ether at $3,175. According to NS3.AI, the financial institution also adjusted its targets for several crypto-related stocks on March 19. Galaxy Digital was an exception, with Citigroup increasing its target from $25 to $28, while maintaining a Neutral rating.
